notes: Friulian: contact with German (Habsburg domination) and Slovene (border zone), plus standard Italian influence.
Ligurian (Genoese): mediterranean trade contacts with Provençal-French, Spanish ports; Italian standardisation.
Lombard: Habsburg + French rule periods (Cisalpine Republic); contact with German Alpine area; Italian convergence.
Sardinian: long Aragonese / Spanish rule (1297-1714) — strong Spanish + Catalan substrate; later Italian.
Sicilian: Norman + Aragonese rule (Spanish); historical Greek and Arabic substrate are NOT in our 13-variety set.
Venetan: Venetian Republic + Habsburg rule + eastern border with Slovene-speaking areas.
